paper_title: Multi4D: High-Fidelity Dynamic Gaussian Splatting via Multi-Level Competitive Allocation

claims.claim: Multi-level Gaussian decomposition (static ℊₛ, persistent ℊ𝒹, transient ℊₜ) resolves tension between motion consistency and visual fidelity

evidence.description: Explicit statement of the fundamental tension and the three-subset decomposition as the solution
evidence.key_excerpt: Instead of a monolithic representation, we distribute modeling capacity across three structured levels: static structure, persistent dynamic geometry, and transient appearance primitives.

evidence.description: Formal definition of ℊ = ℊₛ ∪ ℊ𝒹 ∪ ℊₜ and the bottom-up multi-level competitive allocation framework
evidence.key_excerpt: We represent the scene as 𝒢 = 𝒢s ∪ 𝒢d ∪ 𝒢t: Static (𝒢s): time-invariant 3D Gaussians... Persistent Dynamic (𝒢d): canonical Gaussians driven by a geometric-only deformation field... Transient Dynamic (𝒢t): 4D spatiotemporal Gaussians modeling high-frequency appearance changes.

evidence.description: Ablation confirms both ℊ𝒹 and ℊₜ are necessary: w/o ℊ𝒹 → 32.78 PSNR; w/o ℊₜ → 32.86 PSNR; full → 33.92 PSNR
evidence.key_excerpt: Removing ℊ𝒹 degrades reconstruction quality (32.78 PSNR)... Conversely, removing ℊₜ limits high-frequency appearance modeling, reducing PSNR to 32.86.

claims.claim: Competitive allocation via shared rasterization induces emergent specialization across subsets without pre-assigned roles

evidence.description: Shared transmittance accumulation couples gradients across subsets, forming the basis of cross-set competition
evidence.key_excerpt: The shared transmittance accumulation couples their gradients and forms the basis of cross-set competition.

evidence.description: Natural competition mechanism described
evidence.key_excerpt: Once one subset explains a region, residual-driven densification in the remaining subsets is suppressed.

evidence.description: Visualization and analysis of emergent subset specialization across three diverse benchmarks
evidence.key_excerpt: This emergent specialization arises naturally from the competitive allocation mechanism. Because all subsets are jointly optimized through a shared renderer, each subset competes to explain photometric residuals while respecting its inductive bias.

evidence.description: Visual breakdown showing ℊₛ captures background, ℊ𝒹 maintains coherent motion, ℊₜ activates for fire/smoke/specular highlights

claims.claim: SOTA novel view synthesis on multi-view dynamic scenes (Technicolor 34.30 PSNR, Neu3D 32.30 PSNR)

evidence.description: Multi4D achieves 34.30 mean PSNR, surpassing STG (33.35), E-D3DGS (32.89), and 4DGS (32.07)
evidence.key_excerpt: Our method improves PSNR by 0.95 dB over the strongest Gaussian baseline while achieving real-time rendering at 161 FPS.

evidence.description: Multi4D achieves 32.30 mean PSNR, surpassing STG (32.04), 4DGS (31.57), and DeGauss (31.52)
evidence.key_excerpt: Multi4D also achieves state-of-the-art results on Neu3D with superior reconstruction quality and 217 FPS.

evidence.description: Qualitative comparison on Technicolor shows Multi4D better preserves high-frequency dynamic details
Qualitative comparison on Neu3D shows Multi4D mitigates both over-smoothing (deformation methods) and broken geometry (4D-primitive methods)

claims.claim: Best monocular dynamic reconstruction on NeRF-DS (23.69 PSNR)

evidence.description: Multi4D achieves 23.69 mean PSNR and 0.077 DSSIM, best among all compared methods
evidence.key_excerpt: Multi4D leverages persistent Gaussians 𝒢d for coherent motion modeling and transient Gaussians 𝒢t for localized specular highlights, resulting in consistently stronger reconstructions.

evidence.description: Qualitative results on NeRF-DS: 4D-primitive methods degrade heavily under monocular supervision; Multi4D preserves coherent geometry and fine specular details

claims.claim: Significantly fewer dynamic primitives (25× reduction vs 4DGS)

evidence.description: Explicit comparison: 165k dynamic Gaussians (13k ℊ𝒹 + 152k ℊₜ) vs 4.2M for 4DGS
evidence.key_excerpt: Compared to 4DGS, Multi4D uses only 165k dynamic Gaussians — a 25× reduction from 4.2M — while improving PSNR to 33.92.

evidence.description: Shows dynamic Gaussian count for each ablation configuration
evidence.key_excerpt: Dyn. GS #: Dynamic Gaussians number, denoted as (Persistent 𝒢d + Transient 𝒢t)

evidence.description: 161 FPS on Technicolor (vs 86 STG, 114 4DGS)
evidence.key_excerpt: 161 FPS

evidence.description: 217 FPS on Neu3D (vs 140 STG, 114 4DGS)
evidence.key_excerpt: 217 FPS

claims.claim: SOTA 4D segmentation accuracy (0.9142 mIoU on Neu3D-Mask)

evidence.description: Multi4D achieves 0.9142 mIoU and 0.9952 mAcc, surpassing TRASE (0.8932), SA4D (0.8832), and OpenGaussian (0.8178)
evidence.key_excerpt: Multi4D also extends naturally to 4D Segmentation, achieving state-of-the-art 0.9142 mIoU on the Neu3D-Mask benchmark.

evidence.description: Qualitative: Multi4D produces sharper masks and more stable tracks than TRASE
Method description: semantic optimization on persistent subset only (ℊₚ = ℊₛ ∪ ℊ𝒹), discarding ℊₜ

claims.claim: 10× faster 4D segmentation inference (204 FPS vs 21 FPS TRASE)

evidence.description: Our representation uses only 13k dynamic Gaussians (vs. 624k in TRASE), enabling 32-dimensional feature rendering at 204 FPS — nearly a 10× speedup over the 21 FPS baseline.
evidence.key_excerpt: enabling 32-dimensional feature rendering at 204 FPS — nearly a 10× speedup over the 21 FPS baseline.

claims.claim: Robust to sparse/monocular supervision, unlike 4D-primitive methods

evidence.description: 4D-primitive methods (4DGS: 21.51, STG: 22.54) degrade significantly. Multi4D achieves best 23.69 PSNR.
evidence.key_excerpt: These methods lacking a holistic motion prior degrade significantly and often produce floating artifacts.

evidence.description: Qualitative visualization of monocular NeRF-DS results
Training converges in 1.2 hours versus 5.5 hours on a single RTX 4090
evidence.key_excerpt: Training converges in 1.2 hours versus 5.5 hours on a single RTX 4090 (4.6× faster).

claims.claim: Self-supervised dynamic-static decomposition without ground-truth tracking labels

evidence.description: Self-supervised decomposition based on asymmetric initialization and mask-guided supervision
evidence.key_excerpt: Following the self-supervised dynamic-static separation strategy introduced in [39], we isolate persistent actors from the background without relying on ground-truth tracking labels.

evidence.description: Detailed formulation of mask rendering, composite rendering, and separation loss
evidence.key_excerpt: This decomposition is learned in a fully self-supervised manner through asymmetric initialization and mask-guided supervision.

evidence.description: Pipeline diagram of the self-supervised dynamic-static decomposition

claims.claim: Velocity-aware lifting with momentum inheritance provides strong motion priors for transient primitives

evidence.description: Full description of velocity estimation, momentum inheritance, and period lifting procedure
evidence.key_excerpt: Momentum inheritance provides a strong motion prior for the high-capacity transient subset, mitigating the instability of unconstrained 4D optimization under sparse or monocular supervision.

evidence.description: w/o Periodic Lifting reduces PSNR by 0.70 dB (33.92 → 33.22)
evidence.key_excerpt: Replacing velocity-aware lifting with random initialization reduces PSNR by 0.70 dB.

evidence.description: Implementation details: lifting starts at iteration 6000, performed every 50 iterations until 10000, K=2000, threshold τ=0.05

claims.claim: Mask-aware utility-based pruning prevents temporal over-parameterization

evidence.description: Full description of peak visible contribution scoring with foreground-background-aware gating
evidence.key_excerpt: This foreground–background-aware gating ensures that persistent and static primitives contribute only within their assigned regions, preventing cross-set overlap.

evidence.description: w/o Mask-Aware Pruning increases dynamic Gaussians to 729k and storage by 145%
evidence.key_excerpt: Without the mask-aware utility-based pruning, temporal over-parameterization re-emerges.

claims.claim: Model size reduction: 214.7 MB vs 2.6 GB for 4DGS (12× reduction)

evidence.description: Model size comparison
evidence.key_excerpt: Model size drops from 2.6 GB to 214.7 MB

evidence.description: Storage column shows 214.7 MB for full Multi4D vs 2.6 GB for baseline 4DGS

claims.claim: Subset specialization is robust to hyperparameter variation

evidence.description: Sensitivity sweeps over 5 hyperparameters show at most -0.28 dB PSNR variation from reference
evidence.key_excerpt: Sweeps over regularizers, lifting count, and mask-score threshold remain close to the reference setting, indicating robustness to exact hyperparameters.
